lines.original: Yeah, Aujla
lines.translated: Yeah, Aujla
lines.note: Standard artist self-introduction.

lines.original: Pichhe dekh ke kataar, naddhi karke pyaar
lines.translated: Look behind, there’s a whole line, and this slim girl’s acting all in love.
lines.note: “Naddhi” is slang for an attractive, often slim, girl. “Kataar” means queue/line, implying many people.

lines.original: Kehndi... (haha!)
lines.translated: She’s like… (laughs)
lines.note: Sets up a direct quote, with the laugh indicating a mocking or ironic tone.

lines.original: Hun neend nahi aundi, pachhtaundi
lines.translated: Now she can’t sleep, she’s regretting it.
lines.note: This is the girl's direct quote, expressing her sleeplessness and regret after getting involved with 'jatt boys.'

lines.original: Tu laa ke yaari jattan de munde naa' phire rondi
lines.translated: You linked up with jatt boys and now you’re out here crying.
lines.note: “Yaari” implies a deep, loyal association, not just casual friendship. “Jattan de munde” refers to tough Punjabi/Jatt guys.

lines.original: Ni gaddiyaan track 'te, munde toliyan bana ke, sandh back 'te
lines.translated: Cars line up on the track, the boys roll in packs with bulls at their back.
lines.note: “Toli” means a group or pack. “Sandh” (bull) is a metaphor for strong, aggressive men, here implying powerful backup or a strong presence.

lines.original: Ni thukda nahi modhe ton, spit karaan mic 'te
lines.translated: I don't waste spit on the streets; I spit bars on the mic.
lines.note: This line uses wordplay on 'spit,' contrasting literal spitting (implying disrespect or carelessness) with 'spitting bars' (rapping) on the microphone.

lines.original: Laggi meri mag te main hikkan vich vajjda
lines.translated: My influence has taken hold, I’m echoing in people’s chests.
lines.note: “Hikkan vich vajjda” literally means 'striking in chests,' implying a deep, powerful impact on listeners.

lines.original: Eh bhaunkde aan back 'te, kyon samajh nahi aundi?
lines.translated: They’re barking from the back; why don’t you get it?
lines.note: “Bhaunkde” (barking) is slang for talking trash or gossiping from a distance, implying cowardice.

lines.original: Pata chaundi, tu laake yaari jattan de munde naa'
lines.translated: You’re finding out what it means now that you went and made yaari with jatt boys.
lines.note: “Pata chaundi” implies she is now realizing the consequences or true nature of her involvement. “Yaari” signifies a deep, loyal association.

lines.original: Hun kehndi phire, "Neend nahi aundi"
lines.translated: Now she goes around saying, “I can’t sleep.”
lines.note: This is a recurring refrain, highlighting the girl's public expression of sleeplessness and regret.

lines.original: Chhakkan roti vand ke te vairi rakkhan chand ke
lines.translated: We break bread with our own, and we keep our enemies counted.
lines.note: “Roti vand ke” means sharing food, symbolizing generosity and loyalty within their circle. “Vairi rakkhan chand ke” means keeping enemies few and closely monitored.

lines.original: Bade saanu nind'de te bade saanu bhand'de
lines.translated: Plenty hate on us, and plenty try to stir things up against us.
lines.note: “Nind'de” implies criticism or envy. “Bhand'de” refers to defaming, exposing secrets, or stirring up trouble.

lines.original: Eh yaar mere sandh
lines.translated: My boys are straight bulls
lines.note: “Sandh” (bull) is a common metaphor for strong, aggressive, and loyal men.

lines.original: Te zubaan saambhe yaariyan, te vairi thalle dand de
lines.translated: We guard our word and our friendships, and we keep our enemies under our teeth.
lines.note: “Zubaan saambhe” means to keep one's word. “Vairi thalle dand de” literally means 'enemies under teeth,' a strong idiom for dominating or crushing enemies.

lines.original: Aivein nahi jama launde (launde), na hi aunde
lines.translated: We don't just pull up random or start beef for clout
lines.transliteration: aivein nahi jama launde, na hi aunde
lines.note: “Aivein” means 'for no reason' or 'just like that.' “Jama launde” can refer to gathering a crowd or initiating confrontations.

lines.original: Gale 'ch baanhaan kitthe, eh taan hath nahi phadaunde
lines.translated: They don’t put their arms around your neck; these guys don’t even let you hold their hand.
lines.transliteration: gale ’ch baanhaan kitthe eh taan hath ni phadaunde
lines.note: This emphasizes the tough, emotionally distant nature of 'jatt boys,' contrasting with typical romantic gestures.

lines.original: Tu hath nahi chhadaundi, pata chaundi, tu laa ke yaari
lines.translated: You’re the one who won’t let go of their hand, you want their attention, you’re the one who went and made yaari with them.
lines.transliteration: tu hath ni chhadaundi pata chaundi tu laa ke yaari
lines.note: The singer is blaming the girl for her persistent pursuit and choice to get involved with 'jatt boys.' “Yaari” implies a deep, loyal association.

lines.original: Jattan de munde naa' hun kehndi phire, "Neend nahi aundi"
lines.translated: Now after being with jatt boys, she goes around saying, “I can’t sleep.”
lines.transliteration: jattan de munde naa' hun kehndi phire, 'neend ni aundi'
lines.note: This is a key recurring line, emphasizing the girl's regret and restlessness after her involvement with 'jatt boys.'

lines.original: Hath paye aan udhde, rakaane, baahle rough ne
lines.translated: My hands are tied up with debts, accounts are pending, and the surroundings are rough
lines.note: “Hath paye aan udhde” implies being busy or burdened with unresolved issues. “Rakaane” refers to accounts or tallies, often implying debts or pending matters.

lines.original: Time thode tough ne, utte nu taanhi cuff ne
lines.translated: Times are a bit tough, that’s why there are cuffs on my wrists.
lines.note: “Cuffs” refers to handcuffs, implying arrests or legal troubles as part of his tough lifestyle.

lines.original: Karaai aan palm reading'an, na khed'de bluff ne
lines.translated: I get my palm read, but we don't play bluffing games
lines.note: Contrasts a belief in fate (palm reading) with a straightforward, no-nonsense attitude in real life (not bluffing or faking).

lines.original: Nature'an de jogi aan te jholiyan 'ch sapp ne
lines.translated: We’re yogis of nature, and there are snakes in our bags.
lines.transliteration: nature’an de jogi aan te jholiyan ’ch sapp ne
lines.note: This is a powerful metaphor: appearing calm or spiritual on the surface, but concealing danger or lethal capabilities (snakes in bags).

lines.original: Nehle aale dekhe aa, tu dehle aale dekhe nahi
lines.translated: I’ve seen the nehle types; you haven’t seen the dehle types.
lines.transliteration: nehle aale dekhe ae, tu dehle aale dekhe ni
lines.note: “Nehle” and “dehle” are card ranks in Punjabi slang, used metaphorically to describe different levels of dangerous or sharp individuals. The singer implies he has experience with higher-stakes situations than the addressee.

lines.original: Hun aale dekhe aa, tu pehle aale dekhe nahi
lines.translated: I’ve seen the ones from now; you haven’t seen the ones from before.
lines.transliteration: hun aale dekhe ae tu pehle aale dekhe ni

lines.original: Sohniye, Ghurale ton America mere yaar ne
lines.translated: Beautiful one, my friends run from Ghurala all the way to America.
lines.transliteration: sohniye ghurale ton america mere yaar ne
lines.note: “Sohniye” is an affectionate address meaning ‘beautiful one.’ Ghurala is a Punjabi place name symbolizing his roots.

lines.original: Punjab aale dekhe aa, tu LA aale dekhe nahi
lines.translated: I’ve seen the Punjab ones; you haven’t seen the LA ones.
lines.transliteration: punjab aale dekhe ae tu la aale dekhe ni

lines.original: Naqaab gore rang 'te tu laave gulkand de
lines.translated: On your fair complexion you put on a mask of gulkand.
lines.transliteration: naqaab gore rang ’te tu laave gulkand de
lines.note: “Naqaab” is a covering or mask; “gulkand” is a sweet rose petal preserve, used here as a lush, ornamental image.

lines.original: Vairi nu hara ke karaan chawl, biba, khand de
lines.translated: After defeating my enemies I’ll make you rice with sugar, dear girl.
lines.transliteration: vairi nu hara ke karaan chawl biba khand de
lines.note: “Biba” is an affectionate term for a girl. Rice with sugar is a simple celebratory/sweet dish.

lines.original: Ni taan vi rehnde bhand'de
lines.translated: Otherwise we would still remain empty-handed.
lines.note: “Bhand’de” literally ‘empty,’ often used to mean broke or having nothing.

lines.original: Ni saanu baad 'ch milda, pehlaan ne yaar vand'de
lines.translated: We get ours later; first we share it out among the friends.
lines.transliteration: ni saanu baad ’ch milda pehlaan ne yaar vand’de

lines.original: Mahaul poora wavy, haaye, wavy
lines.translated: The whole atmosphere is completely wavy—wavy.
lines.note: “Wavy” is English slang for a cool, flowing, often intoxicated vibe or atmosphere.

lines.original: Tu paave kaala suit, kade-kade paave navy
lines.translated: Sometimes you wear a black suit, sometimes a navy one.

lines.original: Tu roz azmaundi, pata chaundi
lines.translated: Every day you test me, trying to figure things out.

lines.original: Tu laa ke yaari jattan de munde naa'
lines.translated: You went and got involved with jatt boys.
lines.note: “Yaari” is close, loyal friendship/attachment, often implying romantic or deep involvement here.

lines.original: Hun kehndi phire, "Neend nahi aundi"
lines.translated: Now she goes around saying, “I can’t sleep.”
lines.note: This is a recurring refrain, highlighting the girl's public expression of sleeplessness and regret.

lines.original: Tapaavaan siddha border'an ton round'an diyan gaagaran
lines.translated: I got heat straight from the borders, those round clips
lines.note: Tapaavaan is slang for guns/heaters; gaagaran refers to ammunition containers

lines.original: Na, main na karaan qadraan, na, main na mannan order'an
lines.translated: Nah, I don't give respect, nah, I don't take orders

lines.original: Jitthe raat pai jaave, bichha ke saunjaan chaadaraan
lines.translated: Wherever night falls, I just spread out sheets and sleep there.

lines.original: Ni botal ki shai aa? Laa ke deek pee jaan saagaran
lines.translated: What's a bottle to us? Bring it, we'll drink it like oceans
lines.note: Emphasizes their heavy drinking capacity and party lifestyle

lines.original: Jean'an pauna chhad gayi, chunni 'te laundi sippiyan
lines.translated: She’s stopped wearing jeans; now she decorates her chunni with shells.
lines.note: “Chunni” is a long scarf/veil worn with traditional South Asian outfits; shells are a common decorative element.

lines.original: Sikkhi mere karke Punjabi diyan lippiyan
lines.translated: Because of me, she learned the Punjabi scripts.
lines.note: “Lippiyan” refers to writing systems (like Gurmukhi, Shahmukhi) used for Punjabi.

lines.original: Matthe da tikka bhull gayi, rakaane, mere pyaar 'ch
lines.translated: Lost in my love, dear, she even forgot to put the tikka on her forehead.
lines.note: A “tikka” (or “tikka/bindi”) is a decorative mark or ornament worn on the forehead, often as part of dressing up.

lines.original: Ni kivein yaad kariyan tu bindiyan te tippiyan?
lines.translated: So how could you remember your bindis and little dots?
lines.note: “Bindiyan te tippiyan” refers to small forehead/face decorations, including bindis and tiny beauty dots.

lines.original: Saari na raat saundi gun gaundi
lines.translated: She doesn’t sleep all night; she keeps singing on and on.
lines.note: “Gun gaundi” literally means “she sings,” often with a sense of repeatedly voicing something (like praises or laments).

lines.original: Main hath nahi phadaunda, tu hath nahi chhadaundi
lines.translated: I don’t let go of your hand, and you don’t pull your hand away.

lines.original: Tu booha khadkaundi, pata chaundi
lines.translated: You keep banging on the door, trying to find out.
lines.note: Shows her persistent, desperate attempts to get his attention

lines.original: Tu laa ke yaari jattan de munde naa'
lines.translated: You went and got involved with jatt boys.
lines.note: “Yaari” is close, loyal friendship/attachment, often implying romantic or deep involvement here.

lines.original: Hun kehndi phire, "Neend nahi aundi," pachhtaundi
lines.translated: Now she goes around saying 'I can't sleep,' full of regret
lines.transliteration: Hun kehndi phire, 'Neend nahi aundi,' pachhtaundi
lines.note: The speaker is describing the girl's restless, regretful state after her involvement with jatt boys

lines.original: Tu laa ke yaari jattan de munde naa' phire rondi
lines.translated: You linked up with jatt boys and now you’re out here crying.
lines.note: “Yaari” implies a deep, loyal association, not just casual friendship. “Jattan de munde” refers to tough Punjabi/Jatt guys.

lines.original: Ni gaddiyaan track 'te, munde toliyan bana ke, sandh back 'te
lines.translated: Cars line up on the track, the boys roll in packs with bulls at their back.
lines.note: “Toli” means a group or pack. “Sandh” (bull) is a metaphor for strong, aggressive men, here implying powerful backup or a strong presence.

lines.original: Main thukda nahi modhe ton, spit karaan mic 'te
lines.translated: I don't just talk trash, I spit bars on the mic
lines.note: Contrasts casual disrespect with serious musical expression; 'spit' is rap terminology

lines.original: Laggi meri mag te main hikkan vich vajjda
lines.translated: My influence has taken hold, I’m echoing in people’s chests.
lines.note: “Hikkan vich vajjda” literally means 'striking in chests,' implying a deep, powerful impact on listeners.

lines.original: Bhaunkde aan back 'te, kyon samajh nahi aundi?
lines.translated: We’re barking at your back, why don’t you understand?
lines.note: “Bhaunkde” (barking) is slang for loudly talking, threatening, or making their presence felt, like aggressive dogs.

lines.original: Pata chaundi, tu laake yaari jattan de munde naa'
lines.translated: You’re finding out what it means now that you went and made yaari with jatt boys.
lines.note: “Pata chaundi” implies she is now realizing the consequences or true nature of her involvement. “Yaari” signifies a deep, loyal association.

lines.original: Hun kehndi phire, "Neend nahi aundi"
lines.translated: Now she goes around saying, “I can’t sleep.”
lines.note: This is a recurring refrain, highlighting the girl's public expression of sleeplessness and regret.

lines.original: Yeah, haha!
lines.translated: Yeah, haha!
lines.note: Ad-lib expressing amusement at the situation

lines.original: Jay Trak (Jay Trak)
lines.translated: Jay Trak (Jay Trak)
lines.note: Producer credit/tag
